samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. The parameter i is used to represent the current bit position of the dividend. The parameter j is used to represent the current bit position of the divisor. The parameter quotient is used to represent the current quotient. The parameter remainder is used to represent the current remainder.

Divide the 16-bit dividend A by the 8-bit divisor B to obtain the quotient and remainder.

First, initialize the quotient and remainder to 0.

Second, extract the higher 8 bits of the dividend A to obtain the initial remainder.

Third, compare the initial remainder with the divisor B.

Fourth, if the initial remainder is greater than or equal to B, subtract B from the remainder and set the current quotient bit to 1.

Fifth, update the position of the divisor by right-shifting it by 1 bit.

Sixth, repeat steps 4-5 until all bits of the dividend A have been processed.

Finally, assign the computed quotient and remainder to the output ports.
